BlockDAG P2P Interface Discovery Standard

Pool releases must treat complete libp2p multiaddrs as the only sync candidates. Address class is not a sync option or priority signal.

Firewall

All configured interfaces eligible

bdag-p2p-firewall.service accepts the configured BlockDAG P2P port in host INPUT and Docker DOCKER-USER without an interface filter.

Local node

Direct peer list

update-local-peers.py writes addpeer lists for the active local node through complete P2P multiaddrs with peer IDs.

Operator peers

P2P multiaddrs only

Known peers must be supplied as complete P2P multiaddrs. Legacy address-bucket variables are migration input only and must be normalized away.

Upgrade peerstore

Preserved chain evidence

Upgrade installs parse preserved chain startup logs, write a peer discovery manifest, and apply TCP-open candidates before miner setup.

Firewall Contract

The P2P firewall helper is intentionally broad over interfaces and narrow over the single published P2P port. This lets reachability change without editing firewall unit files, while sync peer selection remains based on measured P2P usefulness.

P2P_PORT=8150
BDAG_P2P_PROTOCOLS=tcp
systemctl enable --now bdag-p2p-firewall.service

If a future node build enables UDP-based discovery, set BDAG_P2P_PROTOCOLS=tcp,udp in /etc/default/bdag-p2p-firewall and publish matching Docker UDP ports.

Peer List Contract

SourcePurpose
/dns4/nodeStable same-compose node path that does not depend on host interface state.
Complete P2P multiaddrsOnly sync candidate format; every candidate must include a peer ID.
BDAG_NODE_PEER_ADDRESSESOperator-supplied bootstrap peers for normal P2P startup.
ops/runtime/peer-discovery-current.jsonInstaller/watchdog evidence file showing candidate sources and TCP-open probes from preserved chain peerstore logs and configured peers.
Measured P2P usefulnessP2P ping, peer freshness, and sustained connectivity determine bootstrap quality.

Operational Boundary

update-local-peers.py --apply recreates node containers only when the active peer env changes. Run it during install or an approved maintenance action. A plain update-local-peers.py run is safe for verification because it updates env only and does not recreate containers.
